message: Jake
Kristy
Günter
My schedule is completely packed.
I was almost brusquely sent away.
but when they relayed that I had something to discuss,
I hear there's something to talk about regarding Miss Dern?
Yes. I came to inform you because a new fact has come to light regarding her.
A new fact, is it? Quite intriguing indeed.
I must clarify, I only happened to have the time to meet you today.
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
the surroundings
the matter of
the issue of
the topic of
the case of
to another place
Save
End the game
a bare, utilitarian office stripped of all waste
In a sense,
there's probably no room more fitting for this.
Most of the bookshelves seem to be filled with medical books. I can't believe they're making patients wait in the examination room while they check books.
There are no patients around. Even if they say they're busy, they likely only do a few counseling sessions a day.
They probably don't think highly of me just walking in without an appointment. Though they’re pretending to be calm, their usual cold attitude is amplifying.
Dressed in an extremely traditional suit that looks just like always, a tie on. If the shirt were white, he could probably attend a funeral.
Sitting in front of him feels like being in a counseling session myself.
I'm short on time, can you tell me without dragging it out?
Since there's not much time, please keep it brief.
What you think is that,
he killed someone?
Why would you say such a thing?
You mentioned something like that earlier, right?
No, I didn't say that at all. You must be misunderstanding.
I'm busy too. Could you please leave soon?
I was worried about this morning's conversation and just visited
her, but she's already okay.
Well, well, what basis do you have for that?
She seemed free of worries about Guy and truly looks just like her old self.
That's nonsense. Even if she looks fine, it's temporary.
No, she's definitely back to normal. It's ridiculous to say her mind is unstable. You'll see when we next do counseling.
But is that really true? She's surely mentally unwell. Emotional scars are not easily healed.
So, that means such past traumas don't exist.
That can't be.
If you say she's had some experience that cleared her mind, I could understand.
Anyway, I can't believe it without meeting her. Such sudden changes are strange.
You're right, I think you'll understand better when you meet her.
Speaking of which, there was a murder case in New York recently.
You can't be serious.
I hope my concerns are just paranoia.
Right now, I'm taking time for Miss Dern. If it's an interview,
please set it for another time.
She's completely back to her old self.
That concern is unfounded.
Even if there was a murder case in New York,
that's irrelevant.
Are you thinking about this in connection with...?
is
Do you think that he temporarily became normal because he committed murder?
He's still an unapproachable man. Is my barging in without an appointment that much of a problem for him?
I hope he’s not planning to charge counseling fees while I’m talking to him.
Let’s get this done quickly before I'm charged the $120 counseling fee per hour.
No matter what,
does he not intend to acknowledge that he’s cured? Are you still planning to squeeze out treatment costs, or...
as you said,
is he only temporarily back to normal and not actually cured?
About him,
it might have been a mistake to talk to her.
Really,
if she thinks he committed murder, it could put her in a terrible situation.
I would appreciate it if you wouldn't come to disturb me too much.
